sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: XDTE ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns CBOE:XDTE
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth analysis of the Roundhill S&P 500 0DTE Covered Call Strategy ETF (XDTE), detailing its investment strategy, key statistics, and performance. The ETF focuses on selling zero-days-to-expiration call options against synthetic S&P 500 exposure to generate weekly income distributions, resulting in a high dividend yield of 36.80%. It also covers the fund's expense ratio, AUM, fund flows, and how its dividends are treated for tax purposes.

feed.title: Terreno Realty Corp. Announces Leases in Gardena, CA
feed.summary: Terreno Realty Corporation announced an early lease renewal for a 2.8-acre improved land parcel in Gardena, California, extending the lease until August 2029. Additionally, the same tenant has leased an adjacent 2.3-acre parcel, with this new lease also set to expire in August 2029. The company specializes in acquiring, owning, and operating industrial real estate in major coastal U.S. markets.

feed.title: Capgemini and McDonald’s extend technology partnership for five years
feed.summary: Capgemini and McDonald's have extended their technology partnership for another five years, focusing on modernizing restaurants and accelerating digital features worldwide. This collaboration aligns with McDonald's "Accelerating the Arches" strategy, aiming to enhance guest and crew experiences and drive revenue growth. The partnership will leverage AI, Google Cloud technology, and Capgemini's Applied Innovation Exchange to support McDonald's digital transformation and operational efficiency.

feed.title: Better Dividend Stock: AGNC Investment vs. Realty Income
feed.summary: This article compares AGNC Investment and Realty Income as dividend stocks, highlighting that while AGNC has a significantly higher yield, its dividend payments are volatile and have been trending downwards. In contrast, Realty Income, a net-lease REIT, offers a lower but more reliable dividend that has increased for 31 consecutive years, making it a better choice for income-focused investors despite the lower yield. The author emphasizes that dividend yield alone is not a sufficient metric for choosing a dividend stock.

feed.title: Costco reports higher revenue and profit in Q2 FY26
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation reported increased revenue and profit for both the second quarter and the first half of fiscal year 2026. For Q2 FY26, total revenue reached $69.59 billion, up from $63.72 billion in the previous year, with net sales growing by 9.1% and membership fee income also increasing. The company's net income for the quarter was $2.03 billion, and it currently operates 924 warehouses globally.

feed.title: Solid Waste Management Business Report 2026: $405+ Bn
feed.summary: The global solid waste management market is projected to grow from $328.9 billion in 2025 to $344.12 billion in 2026, reaching $405.2 billion by 2030, driven by urbanization, increasing waste generation, government initiatives, and environmental concerns. Key trends include organized waste collection, recycling, resource recovery, and waste-to-energy solutions, with Asia-Pacific expected to be the fastest-growing region. Leading companies are adopting technological advancements like waste sorting robots to enhance segregation and recycling efficiencies.

feed.title: E-Waste Management Business Report 2026: $149.96 Bn Market
feed.summary: The e-waste management market is experiencing significant growth, projected to reach $149.96 billion by 2030, driven by increased electronics usage, regulatory advancements, and a demand for recycled materials. Key trends include automated sorting, closed-loop recycling, and refurbishment programs. Europe held the largest market share in 2025, with Asia-Pacific expected to be the fastest-growing region.

feed.title: General Mills appoints new chief supply chain officer
feed.summary: General Mills has appointed Jonathan Ness as its new chief supply chain officer, effective March 16, 2026. Ness, who has been with General Mills for nearly two decades and served as interim chief supply chain officer since January, will oversee global supply chain operations and report to Chairman and CEO Jeff Harmening. This appointment underscores the company's focus on supply chain resilience and operational efficiency amidst ongoing industry challenges.
